REMOVED FROM COURT TRIAL CALENDAR ON MAR-09-2016 IN DEPT. 502 - TRIAL CONCLUDED IN TWO COURT DAYS. THE COURT FINDS FOR PLAINTIFF BOOT HUGHSTON ON HIS BREACH OF CONTRACT CLAIM AGAINST DEFENDANTS INTERNATIONAL CONTAINER SERVICES AND JASON CRUZ, AND AWARDS PLAINTIFF DAMAGES IN THE AMOUNT OF $15,000.00. COUNSEL FOR PLAINTIFF SHALL PREPARE THE PROPOSED JUDGMENT AND SUBMIT SAME TO THE COURT FOR REVIEW. PROCEEDINGS REPORTED BY TAMARA WILLAT, CSR #4609. TELEPHONE: 415-272-1753. JUDGE: A. JAMES ROBERTSON, II. CLERK: KEVIN DOUGHERTY. (D502).

MASTER COURT CALENDAR SET FOR JUL-27-2015 CONTINUED TO MASTER CALENDAR COURT ON NOV-02-2015 AT 9:30 AM IN 206. PLAINTIFF BOOT HUGHSTON APPEARING AS SELF-REPRESENTED LITIGANT MAKES AN ORAL REQUEST TO CONTINUE THE TRIAL DATE TO SEEK NEW COUNSEL. AFTER ARGUMENT AND OVER DEFENDANTS' OBJECTION, THE COURT FINDS GOOD CAUSE TO CONTINUE THE TRIAL. THE COURT GRANTED A CONTINUANCE OF THE TRIAL DATE DUE TO PLAINTIFF'S FORMER COUNSEL, KEITH OLIVER, WHO IS UNABLE TO PRACTICE LAW SINCE FEBRUARY OF THIS YEAR. PLAINTIFFS 2B1 MULTIMEDIA, INC. AND ARTNOWSF, WHO ARE STILL REPRESENTED BY KEITH OLIVER, WILL NEED A LAWYER SINCE BOTH ENTITIES ARE CORPORATIONS. DISCOVERY WILL TRACK THE NEW TRIAL DATE. NOTICE OF THE TRIAL DATE WILL BE MAILED BY THE COURT. JUDGE: CYNTHIA M. LEE; CLERK: R. VELUZ; MATTER NOT REPORTED. (206)

DISCOVERY 302, DEFENDANT ATSI CONTAINERS' MOTION FOR ORDER DEEMING MATTERS ADMITTED AND FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS. PLAINTIFF STIPULATES TO THE MATTER BEING DECIDED BY THE JUDGE PRO TEM. THERE BEING NO OTHER APPEARANCES, THE MOVING PARTY IS DEEMED TO HAVE STIPULATED TO THE MATTER BEING DECIDED BY THE JUDGE PRO TEM. THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: IN ITS REPLY BRIEF, THE DEFENDANT WITHDREW ITS MOTION TO HAVE THE REQUESTS AT ISSUE DEEMED ADMITTED BUT CONTINUES TO PURSUE ITS REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS. THE REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS IS DENIED. IT DOES NOT APPEAR THAT THE PLAINTIFF HAD ANY NOTICE OF THE OUTSTANDING DISCOVERY DUE TO PRIOR COUNSEL'S NEGLECT AND ABANDONMENT OF THE PLAINTIFF AND HIS CASE. IT FURTHER APPEARS THAT THE PLAINTIFF WAS UNAWARE OF HIS PRIOR COUNSEL'S ABANDONMENT UNTIL MID-JULY OF THIS YEAR. PREVAILING PARTY TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE PRO TEM: NOAH LEBOWITZ, CLERK: E. LARNAUTI, NOT REPORTED

Motion For Order Deeming Matters Admitted And For Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Sep-18-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT ATSI CONTAINERS' MOTION FOR ORDER DEEMING MATTERS ADMITTED AND FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS.

Appearances: Paul Mangiantini of the Eagle Law Center (208.938.9500) appearing via Court Call for plaintiff and responding party; No other appearances.

Plaintiff stipulates to the matter being decided by the Judge Pro Tem. There being no other appearances, the moving party is deemed to have stipulated to the matter being decided by the Judge Pro Tem. The Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: In its reply brief, the defendant withdrew its motion to have the requests at issue deemed admitted but continues to pursue its request for sanctions. The request for sanctions is DENIED. It does not appear that the plaintiff had any notice of the outstanding discovery due to prior counsel's neglect and abandonment of the plaintiff and his case. It further appears that the plaintiff was unaware of his prior counsel's abandonment until mid-July of this year. Prevailing party to submit a proposed form of order. Judge Pro Tem: Noah Lebowitz, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Not Reported
